content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 15, 1999


Robert K. Gaultney
Supervising Senior Staff Counsel
Employment Law Unit
Department of Corrections - Legal Affairs Division
Post Office Box 942883
Sacramento, California 94283-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-99-022

Dear Mr. Gaultney:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place.² In addition, this letter is based solely upon the facts presented to us in your letter of January 25, 1999, and in our telephone conversations of February 5, 1999 and March 3, 1999.

QUESTION

1. Would you have a conflict of interest if you were to accept a position teaching a course entitled "P.O.S.T. Management Course for Law Enforcement" at Humboldt State University?

2. Would you have a conflict of interest if you were to accept a position teaching a course entitled "Officer Involved Shooting Investigations," a course co-sponsored by P.O.S.T. and the Department of Justice?

_________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Pursuant to Regulation 18329, the Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

File No. A-99-022
Page 2

CONCLUSION

The Act regulates financial conflicts of interest only. Issues and questions regarding
incompatible positions should be directed to the Attorney General's Office.

The facts provided do not indicate that you are making, participating in making, or
attempting to influence a governmental decision in which you have a financial interest, therefore
you would not have a conflict of interest under the Act. However, the payments from Humboldt
University and the Department of Justice would constitute reportable income under the Act, and
each of these entities could be a disqualifying source of income to you in the future.

FACTS

You are a Staff Counsel III (Supervisor) with the Department of Corrections (the
"department"). You work in the Employment Law Unit of the Legal Affairs Division. Your
primary job responsibilities are to supervise Staff Counsel appearing before the State Personnel
Board, the Employment Development Department, Cal-OSHA, and the Fair Employment and
Housing Agency; to provide legal advice to department personnel specialists and department
managers on employment-related matters; and to monitor and coordinate the defense of
employment litigation involving the department and/or its employees. Pursuant to your agency's
conflict of interest code, you are under full disclosure.

Occasionally, because of your background in local law enforcement, you are called upon
to provide training on employment matters.

You have been approached by the Office of Extended Education at Humboldt State
University and asked to provide a block of instruction on liability issues facing law enforcement
managers in a course entitled "P.O.S.T. Management Course for Law Enforcement." This course
is a joint effort of Humboldt State University and the California Commission on Peace Officer
Standards and Training (P.O.S.T.).

Your salary for the course and any travel reimbursements will be paid to you by
Humboldt University. You would be compensated for your time per current faculty pay
schedules, and compensated for your travel by standard mileage rates for faculty employees.
You plan to report this income on your annual financial disclosure form, and to take annual leave
for the time you are participating in the program, which is approximately four times a year. You
would like to know whether you can accept this position.

In addition, you previously provided, in conjunction with another attorney, a block of
instruction on legal issues in "Officer Involved Shooting Investigations," a course co-sponsored
by P.O.S.T. and the Department of Justice Training Center. This course provides credits to
police officers enrolled in the class, to be applied toward continuing education requirements.

File No. A-99-022
Page 3

You taught this class while in private practice prior to coming to work for the Department
of Corrections. You were asked to discontinue this by your Chief Counsel who was unsure if
your involvement with another state agency might present a conflict of interest. However, she
has asked you to revisit this issue with the Commission since you have been regularly
approached by representatives of the course and asked if you could renew your relationship with
them. During our telephone conversation of March 3, 1999, you clarified that at present you do
not have a teaching offer pending, but that you want to know whether you could accept such a
teaching offer in the future.

In the past, the salary for the course and all travel expenses were paid directly by the
Department of Justice per Standard Contract. You plan to take annual leave on the days that you
would teach this course, which would be approximately six times a year. You also intend to
report the income on your annual financial disclosure statement.

Your co-instructor for this course would be an attorney by whom you were previously
employed. During our telephone conversation of February 4, 1999, you stated that it has been
more than four years since you have been in his employ. In addition, his firm, and he in
particular, were recently placed under contract with the Department of Corrections to provide
legal representation to department employees in conflict situations. However, you are not
involved in any manner with the decision to assign cases to him. Furthermore, there is no
monetary relationship between the two of you with regard to this course.

ANALYSIS

One of the stated purposes of the Act is to ensure that public officials will perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the
financial interests of persons who have supported them. (Section 81001(b).) The conflict of
interest provisions are aimed at accomplishing this purpose by prohibiting a public official from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision in which the public official has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)

The facts as provided do not indicate that there is presently a governmental decision
before you regarding the two teaching positions, therefore, under the facts as presented you
would not have a conflict of interest in accepting either position. Nor, based on your present job
duties with the Department of Corrections, do you anticipate being in a position to make,
participate in making, or otherwise influence any governmental decisions in which it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision would have a material financial effect on either
Humboldt University or the Department of Justice.

In addition, your facts reflect that there is no conflict of interest concern arising from
working with your former employer, your co-instructor for the Department of Justice course.
Your co-instructor has not been your employer for more than four years, thus, he is not a

File No. A-99-022
Page 4

disqualifying source of income to you. As a result, Section 87103 regarding financial interests
would not apply. Furthermore, you are not involved in the decision to assign any cases to your
former employer, since he is retained to represent inmates, and you practice employment law.

You have indicated your intent to report the salary from both teaching positions on your
statement of economic interests. We concur with your assessment that any payments you receive
from Humboldt University or the Department of Justice as a result of accepting either teaching
position would be reportable earned income, since you are under full disclosure.

Section 89502(c) provides that no designated employee of a state agency shall accept any
honorarium from any source, if the employee would be required to report the receipt of income
from that source on his or her statement of economic interests. Section 89501(b) specifies that
the term "honorarium" does not include earned income for personal services which are provided
in connection with the practice of a bona fide business, trade, or profession, such as teaching,
practicing law, medicine, insurance, real estate, banking, or building contracts.

Regulation 18932.2 provides that a person is presumed to be engaged in the bona fide
profession of teaching if:

"(a) The individual is under contract or employed to teach at a
school, college, or university accredited approved or authorized as
an educational institution by an agency of the State of California;
or by an agency of any other state in the United States; or by an
agency of the Federal government; or by a bona fide independent
accrediting organization.
(b) The individual receives payment for teaching a course,
presented to assist in maintenance or improvement of professional
skills or knowledge where the course provides credit toward
continuing education requirements of the pertinent profession."

According to your facts, you will be under contract to teach at Humboldt State
University, an accredited university by the State of California, therefore, the payments you will
receive to teach the course would be earned income excluded from the definition of honorarium.

Likewise, it would appear from the facts provided, that the payments from the
Department of Justice also fall out of the definition of honorarium, because Regulation
18932.2(b) is applicable. The course with Department of Justice would be offered to police
officers for continuing education credits, thus your payments would be considered earned
income, and not prohibited honorarium.

You have stated that you are under full disclosure, thus this earned income must be
reported on your statement of economic interests. We have previously stated that payments
received from a state, local, or federal governmental agency as an independent contractor do not

File No. A-99-022
Page 5

fall into the definition of salary, and are therefore not exempt from the definition of income as
provided in section 82030(b)(2).³ (Riddle Advice Letter, No. A-97-294, and Soldani Advice
Letter, No. A-94-042.)

We must also consider whether the reimbursements for your travel expenses are
permissible and reportable. Payments for travel, lodging and per diem are generally classified as
"gifts" or "income". (See Section 89501(c).) Section 82030 broadly defines "income" to include
any payment received for which you provide consideration of equal or greater value to the payor.
Any reimbursement for travel paid to you by Humboldt University or paid to you directly by the
Department of Justice appears to be "income," because it will be a payment received by you for
which you will have provided equal or greater consideration in the form of teaching the proposed
courses. However, the burden is on you, as the public official, to prove that the consideration
was of equal or greater value than the payment received.

In addition, pursuant to section 82030(b)(2), reimbursement for travel expenses and per
diem paid by a state, local, or federal government agency is not considered income for purposes
of the Act. The payments need not be disclosed as income on your Statement of Economic
Interests. (Rainey Advice Letter, No. A-98-101.)

If you accept a teaching position with either Humboldt University or the Department of
Justice, these two entities will be disqualifying sources of income to you, if you are in the
position to make, participate in making or influence a governmental decision which might have a
material financial effect on either of them. (Section 87100.) Since there are no facts regarding
any such decision at this time, we cannot further analyze your obligations under the Act.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Amy Bisson Holloway
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:ABH:tls

³ Section 82030(b)(2) exempts from the definition of "income" salary which is received from a state,
local, or federal governmental agency.

University?
2. Would you have a conflict of interest ifyou were to accept a position teaching a

and the Department of Justice?2 Pursuant to Regulation 18329, the Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct.
(Regulation 1 8329(b)(8)(A).)

CONCLUSION: The Act regulates financial conflicts of interest only. Issues and questions regarding
incompatible positions should be directed to the Attorney General’s Office.
The facts provided do not indicate that you are making, participating in making, or

you would not have a conflict of interest under the Act. However, the payments from Humboldt
University and the Department of Justice would constitute reportable income under the Act, and
